profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use 250–500 mcg SC once or twice daily (modal planning target ~500 mcg/day, frequently split AM/PM near the injury site or abdominal SubQ). Empty stomach is commonly suggested for oral/sublingual gut-focused use; injectable timing is less meal-sensitive. Protocol snapshot here uses twice-daily dosing over a 6-week on / 2-week off cycle (middle of the 4–8 week on and 2–4 week off bands). There is no FDA-approved human SC label dose; oral Phase I Bepecin (mg tablets) is a separate clinical PK design and does not define these mcg charts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 6-week on-cycle sits in the middle of the 4–8 week block most often cited in research-peptide / DIY and clinic BPC-157 charts for soft-tissue recovery before a planned review or rest; acute injuries are sometimes stopped at 4 weeks, chronic work sometimes extended toward 8–12.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 2-week rest matches the low end of the common community 2–4 week off-window used to reassess healing progress and limit unproven continuous exposure; longer rests (4 weeks) are more conservative and also appear in charts.

citations.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use a loading phase of ~2–2.5 mg SC twice weekly (~4–5 mg/week) for 4–6 weeks, then maintenance of ~2–2.5 mg once weekly. Protocol snapshot here uses twice-weekly dosing over a 6-week on / 4-week off cycle (middle of the common 4–8 week on and 2–4 week off bands). Upper community loading charts reach ~4–10 mg/week total (often still split 2×/week); 10 mg twice weekly is outside modal DIY charts. SubQ is most common (IM optional); systemic distribution means site is less critical than for localized BPC-157 culture. There is no FDA-approved human SC label dose — Phase I Tβ4 IV (tens to hundreds of mg) and ophthalmic RGN-259 regimens are separate clinical designs and do not define these charts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 6-week on-cycle sits in the middle of the 4–6 week loading block most often cited in research-peptide / DIY TB-500 charts for soft-tissue recovery before stepping down to maintenance or a planned rest; total active use is sometimes extended toward 8 weeks for chronic work.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest matches the upper end of the common community 2–4 week off-window used to reassess healing progress after a loading block; many protocols instead taper into once-weekly maintenance without a full washout.

citations.title: Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide. Basic properties and clinical applications

profile.timingNotes: Inject once weekly on the same day each week, with or without food.
profile.cycleRationale: Intended for continuous, chronic administration to achieve sustained glycemic control and appetite suppression. Discontinuation leads to rebound hunger and weight regain.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No planned washout period; continuous GLP-1 receptor activation is required to maintain metabolic improvements, with clinical trials supporting long-term safety.

citations.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

profile.timingNotes: Inject once weekly on the same day each week, with or without food.
profile.cycleRationale: Intended for chronic, long-term administration. Dual activation of GIP and GLP-1 receptors requires sustained presence to reset metabolic setpoints and maintain weight loss.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No planned washout period is indicated. Continuous therapy is necessary for long-term weight management and glycemic stability, backed by clinical trial safety data.

citations.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community injectable protocols most often use ~1–2 mg GHK-Cu subcutaneously once daily (some charts list ~1.7 mg as a “standard” daily draw; advanced community use sometimes 2–3 mg), commonly in the morning for an 8-week on-block. Local injection-site sting or redness is frequently reported. Topical cosmetic use is a different route: clinical/cosmetic literature centers on roughly 0.05–1% Copper Tripeptide-1 products applied daily/nightly continuously — not the same as injectable mg charts. Injectable GHK-Cu lacks robust human clinical trials; it is not an FDA-approved injectable drug for anti-aging. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Community injectable protocols commonly run about 8 weeks on (alternatives include ~30 days or 1–2 months) rather than continuous indefinite use, reflecting limited long-term human injectable data and desire to reassess skin/hair response.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An ~8-week off period is a frequently cited community rest after an injectable GHK-Cu block (other guides use ~2–4 weeks); intended to reassess results and limit continuous systemic copper-peptide exposure — not a labeled requirement.

citations.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data

profile.timingNotes: Inject subcutaneously once daily into the abdomen; rotate injection sites. Daily continuous dosing is the clinical standard — FDA trials administered Tesamorelin 7 days per week with no off-days. A '5 days on, 2 days off' schedule is a community convention sometimes used in longevity settings; it is not a clinically validated regimen for Tesamorelin.
profile.cycleRationale: Usually administered continuously for the treatment of HIV-associated lipodystrophy, as discontinuation leads to a rapid reversion of visceral adipose tissue reduction.
profile.restPeriodRationale: Continuous administration is required to maintain growth hormone release and reduce abdominal fat; clinical studies show no rebound tachyphylaxis but immediate loss of efficacy upon cessation.

citations.title: Long-term safety and effects of tesamorelin, a growth hormone-releasing factor analogue, in HIV patients with abdominal fat accumulation

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning to align with pineal rhythms. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Short, intensive 10-14 day (2-week) courses are sufficient to stimulate telomerase activity and restore pineal melatonin secretion, as observed in clinical trials.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A long 6-month (24-week) rest period is recommended as the telomere elongation and anti-aging benefits persist long-term, and frequent cycles are unnecessary and lack long-term safety data.

citations.title: Epithalon peptide induces telomerase activity and telomere elongation in human somatic cells

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use 5 mg SC two to three times weekly (morning, ideally before exercise), with 4–8 week on-blocks. Protocol snapshot here uses thrice-weekly as the standard community frequency and a 6-week on / 4-week off cycle. Alternate community schedules include 5 mg every 5 days for ~4 doses (~20 days) or minority daily microdoses (hundreds of mcg to ~1–2 mg). There is no FDA-approved human SC label dose; animal mg/kg and sparse human/analog data do not define these charts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 6-week on-cycle sits in the middle of the 4–8 week block most often cited in community and research-planning MOTS-c charts for AMPK / mitochondrial metabolic work before a planned review or rest.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest matches common community off-windows used to reassess energy, training, and metabolic markers and to limit unproven continuous exposure; longer rests (6–8 weeks) are sometimes used but are more conservative than the modal chart.

citations.title: The Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide MOTS-c Promotes Metabolic Homeostasis and Reduces Obesity and Insulin Resistance

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use 200–500 mcg daily (modal ~500 mcg), subcutaneously for systemic anti-inflammatory work or orally for gut-focused work (PepT1 uptake; empty stomach preferred). Protocol snapshot here is once-daily morning dosing in an 8-week on / 4-week off block. Start/tolerance charts often begin near 200–250 mcg; higher-burden community tiers use ~1,000 mcg once or twice daily. A minority run 500 mcg 5 days on / 2 days off. Topical use is formulation-specific (not a fixed mcg chart). There is no FDA-approved human label dose. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: An 8-week on-cycle sits in the middle-to-upper end of the 4–8 week (sometimes 8–12 week) anti-inflammatory research-planning window most often cited in community KPV charts for gut-barrier and systemic NF-κB–oriented work before a planned review.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest matches common community off-windows used to reassess gut/skin symptoms and inflammatory load; shorter 2-week rests appear in pulsed flare protocols, while longer rests are more conservative.

citations.title: PepT1-mediated tripeptide KPV uptake reduces intestinal inflammation

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often mirror Phase 2 SC charts: ~2 mg (conservative) to ~4 mg (modal) once daily for ~28 days / 4 weeks, with 8 mg as an upper trial arm that secondary writeups generally do not treat as superior to 4 mg. Protocol snapshot here uses once-daily dosing over a 4-week on / 8-week off cycle (28-day clinical-course length plus a long reassess window; some charts use 8–12 weeks off before another course). Alternate community schedules include 2 mg 2–3× weekly (cost/spacing) or clinic-style 2 mg twice weekly with possible escalation — less common than daily 4 mg trial-mirroring plans. Ignore outlier blogs that quote microgram-only totals inconsistent with published mg fixed doses. There is no US FDA-approved general neuropathy SC label for research ARA-290 / cibinetide vials (orphan-drug history for sarcoidosis SFN is not a general marketing approval). Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week on-cycle matches the ~28-day SC course used in Phase 2 small-fiber neuropathy / diabetes neuropathy research and the modal research-peptide DIY planning block before reassessment.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An 8-week rest sits at the low end of the common community 8–12 week off-window used to reassess neuropathic symptoms and limit unproven continuous exposure after a short IRR-agonist course.

citations.title: ARA 290 for treatment of small fiber neuropathy in sarcoidosis

profile.timingNotes: Once weekly injection. Based on phase 2 trial data. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Used continuously for chronic obesity management. Combining amylin and GLP-1 receptor agonists requires long-term administration to prevent weight regain.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout period is scheduled; chronic treatment is required to sustain metabolic setpoint modifications and satiety signals.

citations.title: Efficacy and safety of co-administered once-weekly cagrilintide 2·4 mg with once-weekly semaglutide 2·4 mg in type 2 diabetes: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, active-controlled, phase 2 trial

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community charts closely track published clinical-trial titration: once-weekly subcutaneous injection on a consistent day (time of day flexible). Common DIY/trial-aligned ladders hold each step ~4 weeks — e.g. Phase 2-style 2→4→8→12 mg, or Phase 3 TRIUMPH-style 2→4→6→9→12 mg (some targets stop at 4 or 9 mg). Catalog low/typical/high map to start (~2 mg), common mid-high maintenance (~8 mg; 9 mg is a major Phase 3 arm), and max studied (~12 mg). Investigational only — not FDA-approved as of mid-2026 secondary sources. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Trial and DIY community practice treat retatrutide as chronic continuous weekly therapy (months of escalation + long maintenance), not a short peptide on/off cycle — weight curves in trials were still moving at 48+ weeks.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No planned rest/washout in trial protocols; community guidance aligned with GLP-1 class experience expects weight rebound if stopped, so continuous dosing is the default research-use framing.

citations.title: Triple-hormone-receptor agonist retatrutide for obesity

profile.timingNotes: Subcutaneous injection for immune system modulation. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week cycle is standard to modulate immune function (T-cell differentiation and maturation) without overstimulating the immune system.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout prevents immune adaptation and allows the host immune system to function autonomously under its newly modulated baseline.

citations.title: Thymosin alpha 1: A comprehensive review of the literature

profile.customFrequencyDescription: Apply topically once or twice daily.
profile.timingNotes: Apply to target areas on clean and dry skin. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Cosmetic formulations for skin and hair health are designed for continuous daily application to maintain local extracellular matrix support.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout is required as topical peptides have low systemic absorption and require continuous presence to maintain aesthetic improvements.

citations.title: Role of topical peptides in preventing or treating aged skin

profile.timingNotes: Inject every other day for 3 doses. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Intense 2-week senolytic therapy targets and clears senescent cells by disrupting the FOXO4-p53 interaction. Continuous administration is avoided to prevent depletion of healthy stem cells.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 24-week (6-month) washout is required because senescent cell clearance is a durable, long-term cellular reset, and frequent clearing is unnecessary and toxic to normal tissue regeneration.

citations.title: Targeted Apoptosis of Senescent Cells Restores Tissue Homeostasis in Response to Chemotoxicity and Aging

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use 100–300 mcg SC once nightly (planning mid ~250 mcg), 30–60 minutes before bedtime. Protocol snapshot here uses nightly dosing over a 4-week on / 4-week off cycle (common cycled chart; alternate community schedules include 8–12 week continuous blocks, short 2–4 week trials, or 2–3× weekly / as-needed nights). Historical clinical sleep studies used intravenous 25 nmol/kg (~mg-scale for adults), which is a separate design and does not define these SC microgram charts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week on-cycle sits in the middle of community cycled DSIP charts for sleep-architecture work (peptides.org-style 4 on / 4 off; short 2-week trials and longer 8–12 week blocks also appear) before a planned review or rest.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest matches common community off-windows used to reassess sleep quality without continuous exposure and to limit unproven long-term nightly use; shorter rests (2 weeks) are sometimes used but are less conservative.

citations.title: A clinical trial with DSIP

profile.timingNotes: Inject on an empty stomach before workouts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week cycle is capped to prevent severe growth hormone receptor desensitization and tachyphylaxis, which are highly pronounced with Hexarelin compared to other GHRPs.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout is required to restore pituitary sensitivity to growth hormone-releasing signals and allow prolactin/cortisol levels to return to baseline.

citations.title: The growth hormone-releasing activity of hexarelin, a new synthetic hexapeptide, in short normal and obese children and in hypopituitary subjects

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning; monitor kidney health indicators. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week cycle targets apoptosis in white adipose tissue vasculature. Restricting cycle length prevents progressive renal toxicity associated with prohibitin targeting.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An 8-week washout period is vital to monitor renal function parameters and allow recovery of endothelial tissues.

citations.title: A peptidomimetic targeting white fat causes weight loss and improved insulin resistance in obese monkeys

profile.timingNotes: For injectable TRT, keep a consistent weekly total dose and draw follow-up testosterone labs at the route/formulation-appropriate interval. Smaller weekly or twice-weekly injections are commonly used to reduce peak/trough swings compared with large infrequent injections. Topical, nasal, oral undecanoate, pellet, and buccal products have different timing and monitoring rules.
profile.cycleRationale: Testosterone replacement therapy is not a finite performance cycle when used for confirmed male hypogonadism; it is a chronic replacement strategy intended to maintain physiologic serum testosterone while symptoms, hematocrit, blood pressure, fertility goals, and prostate-risk context remain acceptable.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No routine washout is indicated for therapeutic TRT. Stopping exogenous testosterone can allow a return toward the patient's hypogonadal baseline and may require clinician planning, especially when fertility recovery or adverse-effect management is the goal.

profile.timingNotes: Take daily at approximately the same time.
profile.cycleRationale: Used continuously (daily low-dose) or as-needed for erectile dysfunction and benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) to maintain steady PDE5 inhibition.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout is required; low-dose daily therapy maintains stable therapeutic plasma concentrations and has a well-established long-term safety profile.

citations.title: Efficacy and safety of tadalafil once daily: considerations for the practical application of a daily dosing option

profile.timingNotes: Take 60 minutes before activity.
profile.cycleRationale: PDE5 inhibitor administered as-needed. No chronic accumulation occurs with on-demand use, allowing continuous long-term utility.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No structured cycle/washout is needed; clearance occurs rapidly (half-life of ~4-5 hours), preventing chronic receptor adaptation.

citations.title: Safety and efficacy of vardenafil

profile.timingNotes: Oral administration; take with water. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Oral anabolic steroid cycle is capped at 8 weeks to mitigate progressive liver enzyme elevation, lipid profile disruption (HDL reduction), and suppression of endogenous testosterone.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An 8-week rest period is required to normalize liver enzymes, restore lipid balance, and allow recovery of the HPTA axis.

citations.title: Controlled administration of dehydrochloromethyltestosterone in humans: Urinary excretion and long-term detection of metabolites for anti-doping purpose

profile.timingNotes: Oral administration; liver support recommended. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Capped at 6 weeks due to rapid and pronounced hepatotoxicity (17a-alkylation), severe water retention, estrogenic side effects, and rapid HPTA suppression.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 6-week washout (equal to cycle length) is the minimum required to allow hepatic recovery, normalize blood pressure, and begin endocrine recovery.

citations.title: A double-blind crossover trial of methandienone (Dianabol, CIBA) in moderate dosage on highly trained experienced athletes

profile.timingNotes: Nasal spray administration. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 4-week cycle is used to modulate GABAergic transmission and BDNF expression for anxiety and cognitive enhancement without causing habituation.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows central neurotransmitter systems to re-equilibrate and ensures long-term efficacy remains high.

citations.title: Selank anxiolytic activity in clinical trials

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning. FDA-approved for hypogonadotropic hypogonadism and cryptorchidism; use as a TRT-adjunct to maintain testicular function is off-label. This specific off-label regimen is not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Commonly used continuously alongside TRT to preserve testicular volume and intratesticular testosterone by mimicking LH, or used in short fertility cycles.
profile.restPeriodRationale: When used as a continuous adjunct to TRT, no washout is scheduled to maintain constant Leydig cell stimulation; clinical indicators guide adjustments.

citations.title: Low-dose human chorionic gonadotropin maintains intratesticular testosterone in normal men with testosterone-induced gonadotropin suppression

profile.timingNotes: Inject in the morning. FDA-approved menotropins are labeled for female ovulation induction and assisted reproductive technology (ART); use for male spermatogenesis induction is off-label. This specific off-label regimen is not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 12-week cycle of Human Menopausal Gonadotropin (supplying FSH and LH) aligns with the ~74-day spermatogenesis cycle required to restore fertility.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows endocrine parameters to be reassessed and prevents down-regulation of gonadal receptors from sustained stimulation.

citations.title: Efficacy and Outcome Predictors of Gonadotropin Treatment for Male Congenital Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism: A Retrospective Study of 223 Patients

profile.timingNotes: Anti-microbial and wound healing peptide; monitor for irritation. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A short 3-week cycle is standard to address localized microbial or biofilm infections. Capped to prevent pro-inflammatory adaptation and tissue irritation.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout avoids systemic immune activation and allows the microbiome and tissue cells to return to baseline homeostasis.

citations.title: The role of the multifunctional peptide LL-37 in host defense

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use ~250–500 mcg per administration, with multi-dose days (2–3×) preferred because intranasal effects are commonly described as lasting only a few hours. A frequent DIY weekly rhythm is 5 days on / 2 days off within a multi-week run. Russian pharmacy practice for Selanc (not FDA-approved in the US) is widely reported as 300 mcg intranasal three times daily (~900 mcg/day) for a 10–14 day course, with long gaps between courses — different from longer 4-week SubQ-style DIY blocks. SubQ research-use charts often cite ~250–500 mcg/day for ~4 weeks on / 4 weeks off. Prefer consistent morning and evening (or morning/midday/evening) timing; empty-stomach not strictly required. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: DIY SubQ-style community runs commonly last about 4 weeks; Russian clinical-style courses are often only 10–14 days. Catalog default is a 4-week on-block for research-peptide audience flexibility, with notes covering the shorter Russian course pattern.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A ~4-week washout is common after multi-week DIY blocks so users can reassess baseline anxiety/cognition; Russian label-style advice in secondary sources spaces courses by months — longer rests are also used.

citations.title: Selank anxiolytic activity in clinical trials

profile.timingNotes: Inject at night on an empty stomach to mimic nocturnal GH pulse. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 12-week cycle of this GHRH analog mimics physiological pulsatile growth hormone secretion, allowing substantial improvements in body composition and recovery.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout period prevents hypothalamic GHRH receptor downregulation and maintains the pituitary gland's responsive pulsatility.

citations.title: Sermorelin: A better approach to management of adult-onset growth hormone insufficiency?

profile.cycleRationale: An octapeptide that reduces muscle contractions to minimize wrinkles. An 8-week cycle is used to achieve facial smoothing benefits before a maintenance pause.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week rest period allows normal neuromuscular junction signaling to recover fully and prevents receptor desensitization.

citations.title: Role of topical peptides in preventing or treating aged skin

profile.timingNotes: Inject at night on an empty stomach to match physiological secretion.
profile.cycleRationale: Therapeutic human growth hormone replacement is administered continuously to address adult GHD, maintaining body composition and metabolic benefits.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout is scheduled in medical GHD replacement protocols, as continuous low-dose administration avoids receptor down-regulation while preventing metabolic relapse.

citations.title: Growth hormone replacement in adults

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often use intranasal Semax at ~200–600 mcg per administration, commonly 300 mcg once or twice daily (morning and optional early afternoon; avoid late evening). Protocol snapshot here uses twice-daily dosing over a 2-week on / 2-week off cycle (middle of the common 7–14 day on and ~1–2 week off bands; some charts use 10–20 day courses). Upper DIY cognitive blocks may use ~600 mcg 1–2× daily; multi-mg/day Russian 1% acute-stroke product schedules are hospital-context and are not these charts. SubQ charts (e.g. ~0.3–0.8 mg) appear as a minority clinic/DIY alternate route. There is no US FDA-approved cognitive nasal/SC label dose. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 2-week on-cycle sits in the middle of the 7–14 day (sometimes 10–20 day) block most often cited in research-peptide / DIY nasal Semax charts for focus and cognitive work before a planned break; continuous multi-month use is not well characterized.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 2-week rest matches common community off-windows (often equal to on-time, or 1–2 weeks) used to reassess cognition/sleep and limit unproven continuous exposure after a short course.

citations.title: Neuropeptide Semax in experimental models of brain function

profile.timingNotes: Potent Semax variant, nasal administration. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: An acetylated, amidated version of Semax for enhanced blood-brain barrier penetration. A 4-week cycle is capped to prevent neurotransmitter accommodation.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout ensures the neuro-restorative BDNF pathways do not become habituated to exogenous peptidergic stimulation.

citations.title: Neuropeptide Semax in experimental models of brain function

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community SubQ protocols most often use ~50–100 mg per injection two to three times weekly (modal maintenance ~50 mg; upper wellness charts ~100 mg), preferably in the morning. Start low (~25 mg, 1–2× weekly) to assess flush, nausea, or transient chest pressure; inject slowly. Optional short loading phases of 100–200 mg daily for ~7–14 days appear in clinic charts, then step down to 2–3× weekly maintenance — that daily load is not the long-term Typical. Clinic IV infusions (often 250–1,000 mg per multi-hour session) are a different route and dose class and should not be read as home SubQ guidance. Oral NMN/NR charts are separate. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Most community and wellness SubQ charts treat NAD+ as ongoing maintenance (2–3× weekly) rather than a fixed multi-week on/off peptide cycle; optional short loading blocks are followed by indefinite or long maintenance with periodic symptom/lab review.
profile.restPeriodRationale: Hard washouts are not universally required in community SubQ charts because NAD+ is an endogenous coenzyme; many users still take pragmatic review breaks or reduce frequency if side effects or diminishing returns appear. Continuous use is empirical, not proven necessary or universally safe at high dose.

citations.title: NAD+ metabolism and its roles in cellular processes during ageing

profile.cycleRationale: Administered as-needed or continuously for social bonding, stress reduction, or mood stabilization. No accumulation or severe receptor downregulation is observed at low physiological doses.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No structured washout is needed for low-dose, intermittent, or continuous administration; rapid clearance prevents receptor habituation.

citations.title: Oxytocin increases trust in humans

profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHS-R1a) to resensitize and endocrine markers to return to baseline.

profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout period is required to restore ghrelin receptor sensitivity and normalize metabolic appetite signaling.

citations.title: Pharmacokinetics of GHRP-6 in healthy male volunteers

profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout allows pituitary secretagogue receptors to resensitize, maintaining the efficacy of subsequent cycles.

citations.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue

profile.timingNotes: Once weekly injection, long-acting amylin receptor agonist. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Amylin receptor agonist used continuously for chronic weight management. Interruption of therapy is associated with loss of satiety signals and weight rebound.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No scheduled washout period is indicated. Long-term continuous therapy is required for sustained weight loss, with clinical safety trials supporting ongoing administration.

citations.title: Once-weekly cagrilintide for weight management in people with overweight and obesity: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled and active-controlled, dose-finding phase 2 trial

profile.timingNotes: Apply to face at night before sleep. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A topical anti-aging formulation designed for continuous daily application to maintain skin elasticity and stimulate local collagen remodeling.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No washout period is necessary due to localized topical action and lack of systemic cumulative exposure.

citations.title: Role of topical peptides in preventing or treating aged skin

profile.timingNotes: Inject subcutaneous on an empty stomach. Must be fasted at least 2 hours prior and 30 minutes after injection to avoid insulin blunting the growth hormone pulse. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: A 12-week cycle of this GHRH analog mimics the natural physiological secretion of growth hormone, promoting improvements in cellular repair, sleep quality, and body composition.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout period is recommended to prevent receptor habituation on pituitary somatotropes and maintain healthy baseline responsiveness.

citations.title: Pulsatile Secretion of Growth Hormone (GH) Persists during Continuous Stimulation by CJC-1295, a Long-Acting GH-Releasing Hormone Analog
Human growth hormone-releasing factor (hGRF)1-29-albumin bioconjugates activate the GRF receptor on the anterior pituitary in rats: identification of CJC-1295 as a long-lasting GRF analog

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols for CJC-1295 No DAC (Mod GRF 1-29) + Ipamorelin most often use equal mcg amounts of each (commonly 100 mcg each; advanced charts 200–300 mcg each), injected subcutaneously 1–3× daily — with morning + bedtime as the usual twice-daily pattern and a 5-days-on / 2-days-off weekly rhythm. Always dose on an empty stomach (typically ≥2 hours after food and wait ~30 minutes before eating) so insulin does not blunt the GH pulse; the pre-sleep dose is usually prioritized. Neither peptide nor this combo is FDA-approved for any indication (as of 2025–2026); do not confuse No-DAC multi-daily pulses with long-acting CJC-1295 DAC weekly multi-mg clinical PK regimens. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Community GH-secretagogue stacks commonly run ~8–12 (sometimes up to 16) weeks on; Catalog default is a 12-week on-block reflecting the most-cited DIY cycle length for this blend, after which many users stop or rest rather than dosing indefinitely.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A ~4-week washout is the usual community rest after a multi-month run, intended to reassess results and reduce continuous GHS/GHRH receptor stimulation; not a labeled requirement.

Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
Pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling of ipamorelin, a growth hormone releasing peptide, in human volunteers

profile.timingNotes: Inject subcutaneous near the injury site (for BPC-157) or systemically. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Cycled for 8 weeks during active musculoskeletal injury rehabilitation to optimize localized angiogenesis and cell migration, avoiding prolonged activation of tissue growth factors.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A 4-week washout period is recommended to prevent receptor downregulation, allow cellular motility pathways to return to homeostatic baseline, and assess tissue healing.

Thymosin beta-4 accelerates wound healing

profile.timingNotes: Commonly administered subcutaneously once daily, often in the morning on an empty stomach (fasted), as insulin/feeding may blunt lipolysis. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Clinical trials evaluated AOD-9604 over multi-week to multi-month courses; a ~12-week block mirrors the trial duration that showed any (modest) effect before reassessing.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A short break allows reassessment of response and tolerability; there is no validated continuous-use safety profile, so periodic pauses are conservative.

citations.title: Metabolic studies of a synthetic lipolytic domain (AOD9604) of human growth hormone
Effects of human GH and its lipolytic fragment (AOD9604) on lipid metabolism in obese and beta3-AR knock-out mice

profile.timingNotes: In informal research protocols, typically administered once daily (often post-workout) followed by a carbohydrate- and protein-containing meal within ~30 minutes to blunt the acute hypoglycemic response; blood glucose should be monitorable. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Short cycles (commonly ~4 weeks) are used informally to limit cumulative exposure to a potent, long-acting growth/proliferation signal, reducing the window for acromegaly-like changes, organ overgrowth, and tumor-promotion risk. No clinical cycling standard exists.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An off period of similar length allows IGF-1R signaling and the body's own GH/IGF axis to normalize and reduces cumulative oncogenic and organ-growth risk. Anecdotal, not clinically validated.

citations.title: Long R3 IGF-I stimulates organ growth but reduces plasma IGF-I, IGF-II and IGFBP in the guinea pig
Anabolic effects of IGF-I and an IGF-I variant in normal female rats

profile.timingNotes: Injectable protocols use a daily loading phase (~1-2 weeks) until desired pigmentation, then 2-3x weekly maintenance; evening or with-food dosing may reduce nausea/flushing. The only FDA-approved formulation is afamelanotide (SCENESSE), a 16 mg subcutaneous implant for erythropoietic protoporphyria placed every 60 days by a clinician — the injectable peptide used for tanning is not an approved product. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Injectable use follows loading-then-maintenance rather than fixed on/off cycles: ~1-2 week daily loading builds pigment (peaks ~1 week after dosing), then reduced dosing maintains color. For approved EPP therapy, dosing is an every-60-day implant aligned to the implant's release lifespan.
profile.restPeriodRationale: No formal washout is established for injectable use; pigmentation regresses within ~3 weeks off-drug. Given melanoma/nevi concerns, periodic breaks with dermatologic skin checks are prudent.

citations.title: Skin pigmentation and pharmacokinetics of melanotan-I in humans
Afamelanotide for Erythropoietic Protoporphyria
An unhealthy glow? A review of melanotan use and associated clinical outcomes

profile.timingNotes: Once-daily timing is extrapolated from rodent protocols (once-daily ~4-day courses produced effects); the analog's ~23 h duration in animals loosely supports once-daily dosing. None of this is established for humans; no clinically validated timing exists. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Rodent efficacy studies used short (~4-day) courses; a 4-week cycle is a conservative research-use convention, not a clinically validated regimen.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A rest period is precautionary given the complete absence of long-term human safety data; there is no validated continuous-use protocol.

Spadin, a Sortilin-Derived Peptide, Targeting Rodent TREK-1 Channels: A New Concept in Antidepressant Drug Design
Fighting against depression with TREK-1 blockers: Past and future. A focus on spadin

profile.timingNotes: Research-peptide / DIY community protocols most often describe once-daily subcutaneous injection (commonly morning), frequently on a 5-days-on / 2-days-off pattern within an ~8-week on block. Community dose charts commonly cite ~0.5–5 mg per dose for non-Barth use — far below the labeled FORZINITY regimen. Rotate injection sites to reduce local reactions. The only FDA-approved product/dose is elamipretide (FORZINITY) 40 mg SC once daily continuous for Barth syndrome (≥30 kg); that clinical schedule is a different indication and dose scale from community research-use charts. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: Community research-use protocols commonly run a finite ~8-week on block rather than indefinite daily use; cycling is empirical DIY practice and is not the continuous-daily design used in Barth/FORZINITY trials.
profile.restPeriodRationale: An ~8-week off period is commonly described in community guides to reassess effects and limit continuous exposure; this differs from pivotal Barth trials, which used uninterrupted daily dosing.

citations.title: Elamipretide: the first cardiolipin-directed mitochondrial therapeutic for Barth syndrome approved under accelerated approval
Elamipretide: A Review of Its Structure, Mechanism of Action, and Therapeutic Potential
Long-term efficacy and safety of elamipretide in Barth syndrome: 168-week open-label extension of TAZPOWER

profile.timingNotes: Timing is not critical; subcutaneous or intramuscular injection on a fixed twice-weekly schedule (the same two days each week). May be administered near the injury site for localized soft-tissue goals in anecdotal practice. Regimen is empirical and based on scientific literature, including preclinical studies and early clinical research. Not FDA-approved.
profile.cycleRationale: An acute loading block of roughly 4-6 weeks followed by a maintenance or off period is the common anecdotal pattern, mirroring how parent Tb4 was studied as a finite healing course; no validated human cycling protocol exists for the fragment.
profile.restPeriodRationale: A rest period (~4 weeks) is used anecdotally to limit continuous exposure given the absence of long-term human safety data and theoretical concerns with chronic pro-angiogenic, pro-migratory signaling.
